logline: A dessert-based home invasion conscripts two people who've quietly quit on their marriage into the kitchen together — and the dirty victory is that, for one wrecked-window hour, they're on the same side and don't hate it.

beats.title: The Dinner
beats.intent: DECLARE an ironic-narrator title ('The Mahoneys had everything'); PUNCTURE to Greta and Doug eating a gray, weeping casserole in silence. The doorbell rings like a mercy.
beats.feel: cold open — let the silence sit
beats.notes: the inedible dinner IS the marriage

beats.title: The Carol Turns
beats.intent: DECLARE the carolers softening the couple ('real Christmas, how nice'); PUNCTURE as the lyric curdles into a demand — they retreat, kill the porch light, and the carolers do not leave.

beats.title: The Fig
beats.intent: DECLARE Doug's dismissal ('It's kids. Ignore it.') at a midnight crash; PUNCTURE as the carol restarts closer and a second fig lands — a home invasion treated as a noise complaint.

beats.title: The Kitchen
beats.intent: DECLARE 'We are not making these people a single thing'; PUNCTURE to both elbow-deep in flour building a pudding for the terrorists. The thaw is built only through action — the wordless bowl handoff, the spoon takeover, the four-note hum.
beats.notes: loaded objects: the fig-as-projectile, the mixing bowl handed over

beats.title: The Handoff / Dirty Victory
beats.intent: DECLARE the pudding delivered and the carolers gone silent — siege won; PUNCTURE with a callback to Beat 1: they eat the leftovers standing in the wreck, in coats, snow blowing in, and it's the first food either enjoys.
beats.notes: dirty-victory + callback closer; the word 'marriage' never appears
